name: Can I install new hardwood floors over my existing wood subfloor, or does it need to be replaced?
headline: Can I install new hardwood floors over my existing wood subfloor, or does it need to be replaced?

mainEntity.name: Can I install new hardwood floors over my existing wood subfloor, or does it need to be replaced?
mainEntity.text: Can I install new hardwood floors over my existing wood subfloor, or does it need to be replaced?

acceptedAnswer.text: In many cases you can install new hardwood over an existing wood subfloor, but you need to be sure that subfloor is solid, dry, and flat. Start by checking that the existing wood is firmly attached, with no squeaks, soft spots, or sagging areas. Squeaks usually mean loose boards or nails. These can often be fixed by adding screws into the joists every 6 inches or so to tighten everything down. Any damaged or rotted boards should be replaced before you cover them. Next, check for flatness using a long straightedge or level. Most manufacturers want the floor to be within about 3/16 inch over 10 feet. High spots can be sanded down, and low spots can be filled with a leveling compound rated for wood subfloors. Skipping this step can lead to bouncy spots, hollow sounds, or boards that separate over time. Also look at moisture, especially in older coastal homes or over crawlspaces. Make sure there is proper ventilation under the house and a plastic moisture barrier over bare soil in crawlspaces. Too much moisture under hardwood can cause cupping and gaps. Finally, follow the installation method (nail, staple, glue, or float) that matches both your new floor and your subfloor. Do not nail through radiant heat tubes or electrical lines. When in doubt, ask the flooring manufacturer what subfloor conditions they require.
